events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0.125,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Drain slope is under ¼ in/ft — estimated ~⅛ in/ft — which is below the IPC 704.1 minimum for horizontal drainage piping",
    "Insufficient slope will cause solids to strand and accumulate, leading to recurring clogs and potential backup",
    "If any runs are excessively steep, solids can outrun the liquid and strand on pipe walls, also causing blockage"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-support or re-pitch all horizontal drain runs to achieve minimum ¼ in/ft slope per IPC 704.1",
    "Verify slope with a level and tape at each run before close-in",
    "Correct any over-pitched runs that exceed practical maximum slope to prevent solids stranding",
    "Perform a drainage test or visual flow verification after corrections"
  ],
  "rationale": "IPC 704.1 prescribes a minimum slope of ¼ inch per foot for horizontal sanitary drains 3 inches and larger (and ⅛ in/ft only where specifically allowed for certain pipe sizes). The observed slope is visibly under ¼ in/ft, which is non-compliant and will impair drain function by allowing solids to settle and accumulate. The work is roughed-in but not yet closed out, so corrections can be made before concealment.",
  "references": ["IPC 704.1"]
}
